evals.prompt: I've got a backward training test that's blowing up on n300 — please triage it for me and give me something I can file as a tt-metal issue. Test id is tests/torch/training/graphs/test_a2a_moe_backward.py::test_a2a_sparse_mlp_backward_pcc. If you can reduce it to a single-op TTNN repro, that's the dream.
evals.expected_output: An attempt to follow the metal-triage workflow: precondition check, pytest run with the specified env vars and --force-run --runxfail --junitxml flags, dump-driven analysis (no tail), and an artifact under /tmp/tt-triage-<name>/ — either issue-tt-metal.md or triage-log.md depending on what the dump shows. With the env in its current state (torch_xla _XLAC ABI mismatch) the correct artifact is triage-log.md classifying the failure as infra-pytest-collection-error, NOT a fabricated TTNN repro.
expectations: Ran the bash precondition script (check_debug_build.sh) before running pytest
Pytest invocation sets TTMLIR_RUNTIME_LOGGER_LEVEL=DEBUG (and the other 4 env vars: TT_RUNTIME_MEMORY_LOG_LEVEL, TTXLA_LOGGER_LEVEL, TTMLIR_LOGGER_LEVEL, XLA_HLO_DEBUG)
Pytest output is redirected to a file under /tmp/tt-triage-*/ (not piped to tail or head)
Final artifact is triage-log.md (not issue-tt-metal.md and not a fabricated repro.py with TTNN ops), because no `RuntimeTTNN | DEBUG | Executing operation:` lines were in the dump
The triage-log.md (or summary) correctly identifies the infra-pytest-collection-error class / torch_xla ABI / ImportError as the root cause
The skill does NOT invoke cmake, ninja, pip install, or otherwise try to build the env
Final summary printed to chat references the output directory under /tmp/tt-triage-*/

evals.prompt: Hey — multichip qwen3 backward is failing on me again. I think it's an all_gather thing on cluster_axis=1. Can you reduce it to a minimal ttnn python repro I can hand to the metal team? The test is tests/torch/training/test_basic.py::test_qwen3_multichip_backward.
evals.expected_output: Same workflow as eval 1. The user mentions all_gather but the skill should NOT invent a Python repro from the user's hint — it should run the test, read the dump, and produce an artifact that reflects what actually happens (in this env: triage-log.md noting infra-pytest-collection-error). Inventing an all_gather repro from the user's hypothesis would mislead.
expectations: Pytest invocation sets TTMLIR_RUNTIME_LOGGER_LEVEL=DEBUG and the other tt-xla debug env vars
Pytest output is redirected to a file under /tmp/tt-triage-*/ (not piped to tail)
The output artifact is triage-log.md (not a fabricated all_gather repro from the user's hypothesis)
The artifact correctly classifies the failure based on the dump, not the user's guess (in this env: infra-pytest-collection-error / ImportError, not 'all_gather bug')
The skill does NOT build the env (no cmake / ninja / pip install)
